name: Löyly
highlights: Award-winning architecture by Avanto Architects. Smoke sauna + wood-fired sauna + sea swimming. Stunning waterfront terrace restaurant. One of the best löyly experiences in Helsinki.

name: Kotiharjun Sauna
highlights: Helsinki's last remaining traditional public wood-fired sauna, operating since 1928. Authentic neighborhood feel, no-frills experience. Legendary kiuas (stove). The gold standard of Finnish public sauna culture.

name: Sompasauna
hours: Open 24/7, volunteer-maintained (check social media for stove status)
highlights: Legendary free, volunteer-run DIY sauna. Bring your own wood or find some there. Pure communal spirit — strangers tend the fire together. Relocated to Mustikkamaa island in 2025. Nude, co-ed, zero pretension.

name: Allas Sea Pool
highlights: Floating sea pool complex right by Market Square. Three pools (one heated, one cold sea water, one kids). Saunas with harbor views. More of a pool/wellness experience than a hardcore sauna, but the setting is unbeatable.

name: Kulttuurisauna
highlights: Minimalist architectural gem on the waterfront. Wood-pellet heated kiuas designed by the owners. Meditative, quiet atmosphere — no talking in the sauna room. Sea swimming access. Beautiful concrete and wood design.

name: Sauna Hermanni
highlights: Affordable neighborhood public sauna operating since the 1950s. Electric kiuas but proper löyly. Authentic working-class Helsinki sauna culture. No tourists, just locals.

name: Rajaportin Sauna
highlights: Finland's oldest public sauna, continuously operating since 1906. Wood-fired kiuas with exceptional löyly. Located in the historic Pispala district. Pure, unchanged Finnish sauna tradition. A pilgrimage site for sauna enthusiasts.

name: Rauhaniemen Kansankylpylä
hours: Daily, hours vary by season (summer longer hours)
highlights: Public lakeside sauna on Lake Näsijärvi. Wood-fired with direct lake access for cold plunge. Operating since 1929. Winter swimming (avanto) is spectacular. The quintessential Finnish lake sauna experience.

name: Kuuma
highlights: Modern public sauna in Tampere's waterfront. Wood-fired and smoke saunas plus lake swimming in Ratinanvirta rapids. Excellent restaurant downstairs. Combines tradition with modern comfort. Part of Tampere's Sauna Capital identity.

name: Kaupinojan Sauna
highlights: Lakeside wood-fired public sauna with excellent kiuas. Direct access to Lake Näsijärvi for swimming. Quiet, authentic setting. Known locally for having one of the best stoves in Tampere.

name: Mooska Farm Smoke Sauna
address: Mooska talu, Haanja vald, Võrumaa, Estonia
highlights: UNESCO-listed Võro smoke sauna tradition. Ancient technique: fire the stove for 6+ hours, no chimney, smoke permeates the room then clears. The walls are black from centuries of use. Includes traditional rituals with birch whisks (viht). As close to the primal sauna origin as you can get.

name: Centralbadet
highlights: Art Nouveau bathhouse from 1904 in central Stockholm. Beautiful pool, multiple saunas, and spa. More upscale wellness than hardcore Finnish sauna, but the historic building is stunning.

name: Hellasgården
highlights: Wood-fired lakeside sauna in nature reserve just outside Stockholm. Direct lake swimming year-round. Affordable, no-frills, feels like a Finnish public sauna transplanted to Sweden. Ice swimming in winter is incredible.

name: CopenHot
highlights: Wood-fired saunas and hot tubs on Copenhagen's waterfront. Relaxed Scandinavian vibe with harbor swimming. Multiple saunas including a large barrel sauna. Good löyly quality for Denmark.

name: Banya No.1
highlights: Authentic Russian banya experience in Hoxton. Intense steam, birch branch parenie (venik massage available). Three-story building with plunge pools, steam rooms, and a lounge. Hot and intense — different tradition from Finnish but serious about heat.

name: Community Sauna Baths
highlights: Non-profit, community-run wood-fired sauna in Camden. Closest thing to a Finnish public sauna ethos in London: affordable, communal, no pretension. Volunteer-supported. Small but proper löyly.

name: Temz Floating Saunas
address: Various locations on the Thames, London
highlights: Floating wood-fired sauna on the Thames. Unique London experience — sauna on a barge with river views. Cold plunge into the Thames if you dare. Small and intimate.

name: Vabali Spa
highlights: Massive Balinese-themed spa with 12+ different saunas including a huge aufguss event sauna. Textile-free (FKK), outdoor pools, gardens. The aufguss ceremonies are theatrical and intense. More spa than sauna, but the variety is unmatched.

name: Stadtbad Neukölln
highlights: Historic 1914 Roman-style public bathhouse with beautiful tiled interior. Public sauna area with multiple rooms. Affordable, truly public, municipal-run. The architecture alone is worth the visit.

name: Russian & Turkish Baths
hours: Daily (hours vary, check website; co-ed and single-sex sessions)
highlights: East Village institution since 1892. Russian steam room, Turkish room, Swedish sauna, ice-cold plunge. Platza (oak-leaf branch beating) available. Gritty, authentic, old-school NYC. Not Finnish, but the realest bathhouse in America.

name: Bathhouse
highlights: Modern, design-forward bathhouse in Williamsburg. Multiple thermal rooms, cold plunge, warm pool. More wellness/design hotel vibe than traditional sauna. Beautiful space but far from Finnish ethos.

name: Spa Land Centum City
highlights: Premium jjimjilbang inside the world's largest department store. 22 themed rooms using natural hot spring water. Different tradition entirely — you wear provided clothes, sleep on heated floors, eat in the food court. A full-day social experience. Not Finnish at all, but impressive in its own right.

name: Silja Line Ferry Sauna
highlights: Sauna on a cruise ferry crossing the Baltic Sea. Small electric sauna on deck with sea views. The novelty factor is high — sauna while cruising between Finland and Sweden. Not the best sauna, but the most unique setting.

name: Sanduny Baths
highlights: Moscow's most famous banya, operating since 1808. Ornate palace-like interior with marble, gold leaf, and chandeliers. Multiple steam rooms from mild to extreme. Professional parenie (venik massage). The grandest bathhouse in Russia.

name: Tallinna Saun
highlights: Believed to be the oldest continuously operating public sauna in Europe, steaming since 1882. Brick-vaulted ceilings, vintage crown light fixtures, and gas/wood-fired stoves. Separate men's, women's, and mixed sections. A living monument to Baltic bathing culture.

name: Kalma Saun
highlights: Art Deco bathhouse from 1928 in the hip Kalamaja district. Men's side has a legendary wood-burning stove; women's side is electric. Cold plunge pools in both sections, steam room, and a bar. A favorite of Tallinn locals and visiting sauna enthusiasts alike.

name: Raua Saun
highlights: City-run public sauna operating since 1936, recently renovated. Electric sauna and Turkish-style steam room. Affordable and authentic — a working neighborhood sauna in the Kadriorg area. Café Fe attached serves post-sauna drinks and snacks.

name: Heldeke! Sauna
hours: Wed & Sun sessions at 17:30 and 20:00 (2 hours each)
highlights: Unisex mixed sauna in Kalamaja's creative quarter, doubling as a theatre-bar. Cold plunge pool at ~15C, vaudeville-style bar with tribune seating. Inclusive, social atmosphere — the most fun you can have at a sauna in Tallinn. Towel rental €3.

name: Iglupark
price: €24 communal / €110-130 private (2 hours, up to 10 people)
highlights: Five barrel-shaped igloo saunas on the Noblessner waterfront, next to the Seaplane Harbour. Direct sea access for cold plunging into the Baltic. Towels and showers included in private bookings. Stunning harborfront setting with views across Tallinn Bay.

name: Anne Saun
highlights: The oldest and most loved sauna complex in Tartu, operating for over 30 years. Shared general sauna sessions plus private family saunas available for hourly booking. A genuine public sauna serving Estonia's university city.

name: Saunamaa (Sauna Land)
address: Nogu küla, 66263 Rõuge vald, Võru County
highlights: A sauna village in the heart of Võromaa offering 8+ different saunas: smoke sauna, Finnish sauna, cave sauna, forest sauna, an Indian sweat lodge, barrel baths, and hot tubs. Accommodation on site. Perfect for groups wanting to experience the full range of Estonian sauna traditions in one place.

name: Mustjõe Kõrtsitalu Smoke Sauna
address: Mustjõe küla, Anija vald, Harju maakond
hours: Shared sauna Thursdays; private sessions by advance booking
highlights: Authentic smoke sauna at a historic tavern estate in Kõrvemaa, just an hour from Tallinn. UNESCO-recognized tradition with the 'Hell's Cauldron' outdoor hot tub. Includes towels, tea, and spring water. Optional farmhouse dinner. Accessible day-trip smoke sauna experience from the capital.

name: Baltā Pirts
highlights: Riga's oldest surviving public sauna, open since 1908 — the only pirts to survive both World Wars and Soviet occupation. Wood-fired with traditional Latvian pirts rituals. You can book a pirtnieks (sauna master) for an authentic whisking ceremony with birch, oak, or eucalyptus branches.

name: Pirts Nr.1
hours: Mon-Sun 09:00-22:00 (Tue closes 20:30; women's section limited Fri-Sun)
highlights: Claims to house the largest wood-burning sauna stoves in the Baltics. Separate men's and women's divisions with sauna, steam room, Turkish bath, cold pool at 8C, and showers. Professional massage services available. On-site Georgian grill bar 'Šašliks Nr.1'. A modern take on the Latvian pirts tradition.

name: Ivanas Muša Gongą (Central Sauna)
highlights: Vilnius's best-loved public sauna with themed sessions led by professional sauna masters. Two wood-fired saunas, outdoor hot tub, cold-water bath, and a bar. All accessories provided: towels, ponchos, sauna hats, birch whisks, aromatherapy. Multiple locations across Vilnius including a former prison and a pontoon on the Neris river.

name: Pilaitės Malūno Pirtys
highlights: Three unique wood-fired bathhouses on the grounds of historic Pilaitė Manor, nestled along the Sudervėlė stream surrounded by forest. The Old Bathhouse hosts traditional pre-wedding and pre-birth rituals. The Great Bathhouse has a powerful intermittent-firing stove unique in Vilnius, with black oak benches. An authentic Lithuanian pirtis experience rooted in centuries-old tradition.

name: Lampėdžių Pirtis
highlights: Public lakeside pirtis in Kaunas blending nature with traditional sauna. Located in the Lampėdžiai neighborhood by the Kaunas Reservoir, offering traditional sauna, massages, and outdoor activities including bicycle and water bicycle rental. A serene oasis where Kaunas locals go to unwind by the water.

name: KOK Oslo
highlights: Oslo's original floating sauna fleet moored at Aker Brygge with views of Akershus Fortress. Wood-fired sauna boats with direct fjord plunge. Featured in TIME's World's Greatest Places. Private cruises available.

name: Pust Sauna
highlights: A-frame floating sauna platform in the Arctic, inspired by traditional fish-drying racks. Panoramic windows facing the Arctic Cathedral and fjord. Northern Lights sauna sessions in winter. Direct Arctic fjord plunge.

name: Bastuflotten ReLaxa
highlights: Wood-fired sauna raft moored at Stocksund Harbour north of central Stockholm. Cruise the archipelago with sundeck and relaxation chairs. Direct swim in Stockholm archipelago waters. A proper wood-fired stove on the water.

name: Platform CPH
hours: Public drop-in Sundays (1-hour slots); private bookings other days
highlights: Wood-fired floating sauna that glides through Copenhagen's canals with constantly changing views of the city's waterfront. Harbour swimming directly from the sauna. Affordable Sunday drop-in sessions.

name: Von Sauna
highlights: Washington State's first public floating sauna — two stationary vessels on Lake Washington at Carillon Point marina. Wood-fired with stunning views of the Cascades and Seattle skyline. Lake plunge between heat cycles.

name: HAVN Saunas
address: Ship Point, Government St & Wharf St, Victoria Inner Harbour
hours: By reservation (3-hour sessions, book 2+ weeks ahead)
highlights: Built on a repurposed 1943 WWII-era marine barge in Victoria's historic inner harbour. Three saunas, two cold plunge pools, two hot tubs, exfoliation shower, and garden patio — all floating. A full thermal circuit on the water.

name: Watershed Saunas
address: St Omer Wharf, 110 Beach Street, Queenstown
highlights: New Zealand's first floating sauna on Lake Wakatipu with dramatic views of the Remarkables mountain range. Two custom-built pontoon saunas. Wood-fired with direct lake plunge into glacial waters.

name: HotBoat Sauna
highlights: Scotland's first floating sauna, cruising the tranquil waters of Loch Tay through the Scottish Highlands with a dedicated skipper. Wood-fired heat with bracing Highland loch swims. Stunning mountain scenery.

name: Warm Hearts Floating Sauna
highlights: The world's first floating sauna fuelled by kindness — you pay with a good deed instead of cash. Wood-fired sauna on Amsterdam's Sloterplas lake with direct lake swimming. A unique social experiment in urban wellness.

name: Sauna Seebad Enge
highlights: Three wood-fired sauna barrels floating on a platform directly on Lake Zurich. After sweating, jump straight into the lake (4-6 C in winter). Stunning views of the Alps and city skyline. A beloved Zurich institution combining Badekultur with Nordic sauna tradition.
waterAccess: Lake Zurich (direct plunge from platform)

name: Saunaboot Zurichsee
highlights: Switzerland's first electric-propelled sauna boat with a wood-fired sauna stove. Steer yourself across 34 square miles of Lake Zurich and cold-plunge wherever you like. No skipper's license required. Minimum age 18.
waterAccess: Lake Zurich (plunge anywhere on the lake)

name: Hurlimannbad & Spa Zurich
highlights: Iconic rooftop infinity pool with panoramic city views, housed in a converted 19th-century Hurlimann brewery. Fresh thermal water from Zurich's Aqui source at 35-41 C. Includes a Roman-Irish spa ritual circuit. One of Europe's most architecturally striking urban thermal baths.
waterAccess: Thermal pools and rooftop infinity pool (no natural water)

name: Stadtbad Zurich Hammam & Spa
highlights: Subterranean Turkish-style hammam in the historic Volkshaus building. Features a navel stone, steam bath, mud baths, Kneipp bath, and mist shower. Wednesdays women-only. A true urban oasis hidden beneath one of Zurich's iconic cultural venues.
waterAccess: Indoor plunge pools and Kneipp bath

name: Sauna am See Uster
highlights: Three wood-fired sauna barrels at the Strandbad on Greifensee lake. Direct lake access for cold plunging. Reception yurt with Swedish stove, bistro serving soups and drinks. A rustic, communal lakeside experience 30 minutes from Zurich.
waterAccess: Greifensee lake (direct access for cold plunge)

name: Bains des Paquis
highlights: Legendary Geneva institution on a pier jutting into Lake Geneva with the Jet d'Eau in view. Sauna, hammam, and Turkish bath in winter; open-water swimming in summer. Beloved by locals for its affordable, no-nonsense community spirit and iconic fondue evenings.
waterAccess: Lake Geneva (Lac Leman) direct swimming access

name: Bains des Rives (Bellerive)
highlights: Seasonal winter lakeside pop-up with two saunas housed in yurts at the Bellerive swimming pool on Lac Leman. Nordic-style ambiance with warm wood scents. The contrast between sauna heat and cold lake plunge is the main attraction. Ephemeral and atmospheric.
waterAccess: Lake Geneva (Lac Leman) direct cold plunge

name: Aquabasilea
highlights: Switzerland's largest sauna world with 12 saunas and steam baths across 13,000 m2, including a snow grotto with real snowfall, Switzerland's largest hammam, outdoor sauna houses, salt water grotto, and a schnapps distillery sauna at 75 C. Massive variety near Basel.
waterAccess: Indoor plunge pools, ice fountains, salt pool

name: Sole Uno Wellness World
highlights: Saltwater wellness world with saline pools at 12% salinity, a Russian banya, Finnish earth and fire sauna, bio-sauna, aroma steam baths, and salt water inhalation. Set in a park landscape near the Rhine. The salt concentration creates a Dead-Sea-like floating sensation.
waterAccess: Saltwater pools, outdoor adventure pools

name: Sauna Lorrainebad (Loyly)
highlights: Rustic open-air wood-fired sauna on the banks of the Aare river. Two sauna yurts heated by crackling wood fires, with a reception yurt and relaxation yurts. Drop into the freezing Aare after sweating. Volunteer-run community spirit, no reservations. The closest thing to Finnish public sauna culture in Switzerland.
waterAccess: Aare river (direct cold plunge access)

name: Bernaqua Erlebnisbad & Wellness
highlights: Large wellness complex inside the architecturally striking Westside shopping center designed by Daniel Libeskind. Multiple saunas, Roman-Irish bath ritual, water park with slides. A modern, family-friendly urban wellness destination combining fun and relaxation.
waterAccess: Indoor pools and water park (no natural water)

name: Solbad & Spa Schonbuhl
highlights: Switzerland's largest natural saltwater bath, fed by underground brine springs. Finnish sauna, bio sauna, herbal steam bath, and earth-and-fire sauna. The mineral-rich salt water has therapeutic properties and creates a remarkable floating experience.
waterAccess: Natural saltwater bath, indoor pools

name: Tamina Therme
highlights: Seven saunas across 1,900 m2 including the largest aufguss (infusion) sauna in Switzerland, built from Scandinavian Kelo wood. Thermal water from the historic Tamina Gorge has been used since 1242. Latvian Pirts rituals offered. Eight thermal pools spanning 7,300 m2.
waterAccess: Eight thermal pools fed by Tamina Gorge spring water

name: Leukerbad Therme
highlights: The largest alpine thermal bath in Europe with 10 pools (28-43 C) at 1,411 m altitude. Textile-free sauna area named after surrounding mountains, with views of the Daubenhorn massif. Fed by natural hot springs producing 3.9 million liters daily. Dramatic mountain amphitheater setting.
waterAccess: 10 thermal pools, indoor and outdoor

name: Walliser Alpentherme & Spa
highlights: Upscale thermal spa on Leukerbad's historic village square at 1,400 m. The Valais Sauna Village spans 300+ m2 with themed saunas. Roman-Irish bath ritual with soap brush massage. Panoramic mountain views from outdoor thermal pools. A more intimate, refined alternative to the larger Leukerbad Therme next door.
waterAccess: Thermal pools, indoor and outdoor

name: Les Bains de Lavey
highlights: The hottest thermal spring in Switzerland (69 C at source), nestled in the Rhone valley between mountains and forest. Saunas, steam rooms, and multiple thermal pools surrounded by trees. Accessible from Montreux and Lausanne. A serene mountain retreat with therapeutic mineral waters.
waterAccess: Thermal pools, outdoor pools near the Rhone river

name: Les Bains de la Gruyere
highlights: Alpine spa in the heart of Gruyere country with three pools heated to 34 C by natural mineral springs. Saunas, steam rooms, and fitness area with panoramic views of the Pre-Alps. After bathing, visit a local fromagerie for fondue. Combines wellness with Fribourg's famous gastronomy.
waterAccess: Thermal mineral pools, indoor and outdoor

name: Bogn Engiadina
highlights: Engadine mineral bath in the Romansh-speaking Lower Engadin valley. Separate textile-free sauna area and a Roman-Irish bath ritual circuit. Rich mineral water from local springs. Outdoor whirlpool with views of Engadin mountains. A unique intersection of Romansh culture and Alpine bathing tradition.
waterAccess: Mineral pools, outdoor whirlpool, plunge pools

name: Mineralbad & Spa Rigi Kaltbad
highlights: Designed by Swiss star architect Mario Botta, perched at 1,450 m on Mount Rigi above Lake Lucerne. Combines 600 years of bathing tradition with bold modern architecture. Crystal spa, organic sauna, mountain herbal steam bath, and outdoor mineral pool with staggering views of Pilatus and the Alps. Accessible only by cogwheel train.
waterAccess: Mineral pool (no natural water plunge)

name: Saunaboot Lake Lucerne
highlights: Self-steered sauna boat named Berti on Lake Lucerne (Vierwaldstattersee), built from sustainable Accoya wood. Wood-fired stove heats to 90 C. No skipper's license needed. Cruise to a quiet bay with mountain views, then plunge into the lake surrounded by Alpine scenery. Pure floating wellness.
waterAccess: Lake Lucerne (Vierwaldstattersee) direct plunge

name: Therme Zurzach
highlights: 14 saunas and steam baths — one of the largest sauna landscapes in Switzerland. Features a textile-free bore-rig sauna park, intensive brine bath, cool natural swimming pool, and an elegant bar and lounge. Thermal water from deep underground sources. A serious sauna destination in the Aargau countryside.
waterAccess: Thermal pools, brine bath, natural swimming pool

name: Termali Salini & Spa
hours: Daily ~09:00-21:30 (closed mid-Jun to early Jul for maintenance)
highlights: The largest saltwater pool in Ticino at approximately 400 m2, in the Italian-speaking region of Switzerland. Multiple saunas, spa treatments, and gastronomy under one roof. Mediterranean atmosphere with palm trees and mild Lago Maggiore climate. A taste of la dolce vita meets Alpine wellness.
waterAccess: Saltwater pool, thermal pools

name: Ovaverva Hallenbad, Spa & Sportzentrum
highlights: St. Moritz's public spa and sport center with Kneipp and steam baths, bio sauna, Finnish sauna, and a ladies-only spa. Continuing a wellness tradition in the Engadin that dates back to the Bronze Age mineral springs. Affordable public wellness in one of the world's most exclusive mountain resorts. Note: undergoing major renovation in 2025-2026.
waterAccess: Indoor pools, Kneipp baths

name: eau-la-la Wellness & Erlebnisbad
highlights: Davos's public wellness center at 1,560 m altitude with a log cabin sauna (wood-fired), Finnish sauna, bio sauna, and a rooftop open-air whirlpool. Steam bath and Kneipp facilities. Textile-free sauna zone. An affordable public option in a town better known for the World Economic Forum than for sweating.
waterAccess: Indoor/outdoor pools, rooftop whirlpool

name: Tschuggen Bergoase
price: Day spa pricing on request (luxury tier)
highlights: 5,000 m2 spa designed by Mario Botta, built entirely into the mountainside across four floors. Glass sail structures pierce the mountain to flood the interior with natural light. Multiple saunas, steam baths, pools, and 12 treatment rooms. An architectural masterpiece merging wellness with Alpine landscape at 1,800 m.
waterAccess: Indoor and outdoor pools (no natural water)
